questions.text: What is the primary warning given in the very first Ayah of Surah An-Nahl?
questions.answer: That the command (judgment) of Allah is coming, so do not seek to hasten it.

questions.text: What does 'An-Nahl' mean and why is it a significant title for this Surah?
questions.answer: It means 'The Bee'. It is significant because Allah highlights the bee's inspiration and the medicinal value of honey as signs of His wisdom and providence.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the creation of man in this Surah?
questions.answer: He created man from a sperm-drop, yet man becomes an open adversary.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the purpose of cattle for mankind?
questions.answer: For warmth, numerous benefits, food, and as a source of beauty and transport.

questions.text: What are the benefits of horses, mules, and donkeys according to the Surah?
questions.answer: For you to ride them and as an adornment; and He creates that which you do not know.

questions.text: Who is responsible for showing the straight way according to the Surah?
questions.answer: To Allah belongs the showing of the straight way; and among them are those which are devious.

questions.text: What are the various benefits of rain mentioned in the early verses?
questions.answer: It provides water to drink and for the growth of trees and crops like olives, date-palms, grapes, and every kind of fruit.

questions.text: What celestial bodies has Allah subjected to serve mankind?
questions.answer: The night, the day, the sun, the moon, and the stars.

questions.text: What is the benefit of the sea as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: To eat fresh meat from it and to extract ornaments (pearls/coral) to wear, and for ships to sail through it for commerce.

questions.text: What is the 'sign' mentioned regarding the mountains in this Surah?
questions.answer: They are placed as firm stabilizers (rawasiya) so that the earth does not shake with its inhabitants.

questions.text: What purpose do landmarks and stars serve according to the Surah?
questions.answer: By them, people are guided in their journeys.

questions.text: In this Surah, what is said about counting the favors of Allah?
questions.answer: If you try to count them, you will never be able to number them.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the reaction of the arrogant when told about the Quran?
questions.answer: They dismissively say, 'Legends of the former peoples' (Asatirul Awwalin).

questions.text: What happens to the 'buildings' or plans of those who plotted against the truth in the past?
questions.answer: Allah struck their foundation, and the roof fell upon them from above them, and the punishment came to them from where they did not perceive.

questions.text: What do the angels say to the righteous ones when taking their souls?
questions.answer: They say, 'Peace be upon you (Salamun Alaikum). Enter Paradise for what you used to do.'

questions.text: What was the mission of every messenger sent to every nation?
questions.answer: To call people to 'Worship Allah and avoid Taghut (false deities/evil).'

questions.text: To whom should people ask if they do not know religious matters?
questions.answer: To the 'People of the Reminder' (Ahlul Dhikr/scholars).

questions.text: What is the warning for those who feel secure against the sudden punishment of Allah?
questions.answer: They are warned that the earth could swallow them, or punishment could seize them in the midst of their activities, or seize them by gradual wasting.

questions.text: How is the 'Prostration of Shadows' described in the context of nature?
questions.answer: The shadows of things created by Allah incline to the right and left, prostrating to Allah in humility.

questions.text: Does Allah allow the belief in two gods?
questions.answer: No, Allah has said, 'Do not take for yourselves two deities. He is only one God.'

questions.text: To whom do we turn when we are touched by adversity?
questions.answer: To Allah alone we cry for help.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the reaction of the pagan Arabs to the birth of a daughter?
questions.answer: Their faces would turn black with grief and hidden shame, and they would consider whether to keep her with humiliation or bury her alive.

questions.text: If Allah were to punish people immediately for their wrongdoing, would any creature be left?
questions.answer: No, He would not leave upon the earth any creature, but He defers them for a specified term.

questions.text: What is the sign of Allah's power in the provision of water for a dead land?
questions.answer: He sends down rain from the sky and gives life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness.

questions.text: What is the 'Milk' sign mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Pure milk coming from between the contents of the stomach and blood, palatable to the drinkers.

questions.text: What are the two products mentioned from the fruits of palm trees and grapevines?
questions.answer: Intoxicants (before prohibition) and good provision (like juice and dried fruit).

questions.text: What does the 'Word of Allah' represent for the bees?
questions.answer: It represents divine inspiration (Wahyan) to build hives in mountains, trees, and buildings.

questions.text: What flows from the bellies of bees and what is its benefit?
questions.answer: A drink (honey) of varying colors, which is a healing for mankind.

questions.text: What happens to a person when they reach the 'most abject' stage of old age?
questions.answer: They become like a child, knowing nothing after having had knowledge.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the difference in provision among people as a test?
questions.answer: He has favored some over others in provision; those who are favored should not be ungrateful or refuse to share with their dependents.

questions.text: What parable is used for the slave and his master to explain the concept of Shirk?
questions.answer: The comparison between a slave who has no power and a free man whom Allah has provided for; they are not equal, so how can the Creator be equal to His creation?

questions.text: What parable is used for the 'dumb' (helpless) man and the one who commands justice?
questions.answer: A man who is a burden to his master vs a man who is on the straight path and commands justice.

questions.text: How quickly will the 'Command of the Hour' arrive?
questions.answer: It is but as a blink of an eye or even nearer.

questions.text: In what state does a baby come out of its mother's womb?
questions.answer: Knowing nothing, but Allah gives it hearing, sight, and intellect (hearts) so it may be grateful.

questions.text: What keeps the birds held up in the air according to the Surah?
questions.answer: None holds them up except Allah; indeed, in that are signs for a people who believe.

questions.text: What are the benefits of the skins (hides) and wool of cattle mentioned here?
questions.answer: For making tents that are light to carry, and for furnishings and enjoyment for a time.

questions.text: What has Allah provided for protection from heat and in battle?
questions.answer: Shades from trees/mountains, garments to protect from heat, and coats of mail (armor) to protect in battle.

questions.text: What will happen on the Day of Resurrection when every nation's witness (Messenger) is brought forth?
questions.answer: The disbelievers will not be allowed to make excuses, nor will they be asked to appease Allah.

questions.text: Which three things does Allah command in the famous 'Ayah of Justice' (a specific ayah)?
questions.answer: Justice (Adl), doing good (Ihsan), and giving to kinsfolk.

questions.text: Which three things does Allah forbid in a specific ayah?
questions.answer: Immorality (Fahsha), bad conduct (Munkar), and oppression (Baghy).

questions.text: What is the command regarding fulfilling covenants and oaths?
questions.answer: Fulfill the covenant of Allah when you have taken it and do not break oaths after their confirmation.

questions.text: What parable is used for someone who breaks their oath after making it firm?
questions.answer: A woman who breaks her thread into pieces after she has spun it strong.

questions.text: What reward is promised to those who do righteous deeds, whether male or female, while being believers?
questions.answer: Allah will surely cause them to live a good life and will reward them according to the best of what they used to do.

questions.text: What should a person do before reciting the Quran?
questions.answer: Seek refuge in Allah from Shaitan, the rejected.

questions.text: Does Shaitan have authority over the true believers?
questions.answer: No, he has no authority over those who believe and rely upon their Lord.

questions.text: Who brought down the Quran from the Lord, and for what purpose?
questions.answer: The Holy Spirit (Jibril) brought it down with truth to make firm those who believe and as guidance and glad tidings.

questions.text: How did the polytheists try to explain the source of the Prophet's knowledge?
questions.answer: They said, 'It is only a human being who teaches him,' but Allah points out that the person they refer to speaks a foreign language, while the Quran is in clear Arabic.

questions.text: What is the condition for a person to be forgiven if they utter words of disbelief under duress?
questions.answer: If they are forced while their heart remains content and firm in faith.

questions.text: Describe the example of the city that was safe and secure but then became ungrateful.
questions.answer: It received its provision in abundance from every side, but it denied the favors of Allah, so Allah made it taste the extreme of hunger and fear.

questions.text: What foods are forbidden (Haram) as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Dead animals, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been dedicated to other than Allah.

questions.text: Is there an exception for someone who is forced by necessity to eat forbidden food?
questions.answer: Yes, if they are forced by necessity and not desiring it or transgressing, then Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.

questions.text: How is Prophet Ibrahim (AS) described in this Surah?
questions.answer: As an 'Ummah' (a nation/leader) in himself, devoutly obedient to Allah and a Hanif (monotheist).

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) primary quality regarding Allah's favors?
questions.answer: He was grateful for His favors.

questions.text: What is the 'Sabbath' ruling mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: It was only made obligatory for those who differed over it; it was a test for the Jews.

questions.text: What are the three ways to call people to the path of Allah?
questions.answer: With wisdom (Hikmah), good instruction (Maw'idhatul Hasanah), and by arguing in the best manner.

questions.text: What is the limit of retaliation if one chooses to respond to an injury?
questions.answer: Retaliation should be with the like of that with which one was harmed, though patience is better.

questions.text: How should the Prophet (SAW) handle the plots of the disbelievers?
questions.answer: He is told to be patient and not be in distress because of what they plot.

questions.text: With whom is Allah's support according to the the closing ayah?
questions.answer: With those who fear Him and those who do good (Muhsinun).

questions.text: What is the 'Specified Term' (Ajal) for every person?
questions.answer: The fixed time of death which cannot be advanced or delayed.

questions.text: What is the 'Two Gods' warning?
questions.answer: A refutation of dualism and an affirmation of absolute monotheism.

questions.text: What is the 'Adversary' nature of man mentioned?
questions.answer: Despite his humble origin, man becomes argumentative against his Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Beauty' in cattle?
questions.answer: The sight of them when they are driven home in the evening and out to pasture in the morning.

questions.text: What is the 'Heavy Load' cattle carry for us?
questions.answer: Our burdens and belongings to lands we could not reach except with great difficulty.

questions.text: What is the 'Guidance' mentioned at the beginning?
questions.answer: Allah's role in directing the creation toward their sustenance and purpose.

questions.text: What is the 'Sign' in the sea?
questions.answer: The ability to sail and extract resources like food and ornaments.

questions.text: What is the 'Stabilizer' (Rawasiya)?
questions.answer: The mountains that keep the earth's crust from shaking.

questions.text: What is the 'Praiseworthy' (Al-Hamid)?
questions.answer: Allah, who deserves all thanks for His gifts.

questions.text: What is the 'Burden of Sins' on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The responsibility for one's own sins and the sins of those they led astray.

questions.text: What is the 'Good Life' (Hayatan Tayyibah)?
questions.answer: A life of contentment, faith, and peace in this world.

questions.text: What is the 'Holy Spirit' (Ruh-ul-Qudus)?
questions.answer: The Angel Jibril (Gabriel).

questions.text: What is the 'Arabic Tongue'?
questions.answer: The clear and eloquent language in which the Quran was revealed.

questions.text: What is the 'Hunger and Fear' punishment?
questions.answer: The loss of security and sustenance as a consequence of ungratefulness.

questions.text: What is the 'Nation' (Ummah) in the context of Ibrahim?
questions.answer: That he was a leader and a model for all believers.

questions.text: What is the 'Wisdom' in calling to Allah?
questions.answer: Using the appropriate approach and evidence for each individual.

questions.text: What is the 'Good Instruction'?
questions.answer: Advice that is gentle, persuasive, and beneficial.

questions.text: What is the 'Best Manner' of arguing?
questions.answer: Being respectful, calm, and focused on the truth rather than winning.

questions.text: What is the 'Patience' of the Prophet?
questions.answer: His endurance of the disbelievers' plots and mockery.

questions.text: What is the 'Witness' of the Messenger?
questions.answer: The testimony the Prophet will give regarding his nation's response to the message.

questions.text: What is the 'Healing' in honey?
questions.answer: The physical medicinal properties Allah has placed in what the bees produce.

questions.text: What is the 'Reminder' (Dhikr)?
questions.answer: The Quran and the knowledge of previous revelations.
